title: Browser Security
text: Browser Security Web browsers are the clients in a web application, responsible for sending and receiving data to and from the server. The browser interprets and displays HTML, and makes use of interpreters for programming scripts such as JavaScript, PHP, and Flash. It is therefore vulnerable to attacks that intercept and read the data it sends, or cross-site scripting attacks such as site forgery. Threats Eavesdropping —monitoring and logging the data exchange between the client and server by an intermediate server or sniffing software. Cross-Site Request Forgery —a malicious script or URL sends an HTTP request to an external site for which the user is currently authenticated. Data Leaks —data leaks can provide information that is subsequently used for malicious purposes. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
